Symptoms: Increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, blurred vision, slow healing wounds
Description: A chronic condition affecting how the body metabolizes sugar (glucose). The body either doesn't produce enough insulin or doesn't use insulin effectively.
Treatment: Lifestyle changes (diet and exercise), Metformin, insulin therapy, blood sugar monitoring
Cause: Insulin resistance, genetic factors, obesity, physical inactivity, poor diet
Prevention: Maintain healthy weight, exercise regularly, eat balanced diet, avoid sugary foods, regular blood sugar screening

Symptoms: Headache, shortness of breath, nosebleeds, flushing, dizziness (often asymptomatic)
Description: High blood pressure — a chronic condition where the force of blood against artery walls is consistently too high. Often called the silent killer.
Treatment: ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, diuretics, calcium channel blockers, lifestyle changes
Cause: High sodium diet, obesity, stress, lack of exercise, genetics, kidney disease
Prevention: Reduce sodium intake, exercise regularly, manage stress, limit alcohol, maintain healthy weight

Symptoms: Severe throbbing headache, nausea, vomiting, sensitivity to light and sound, visual aura
Description: A neurological disease characterized by recurrent moderate-to-severe headaches, often on one side of the head.
Treatment: Triptans, pain relievers, anti-nausea medications, preventive medications like beta-blockers
Cause: Hormonal changes, stress, certain foods, sensory stimuli, sleep changes, weather changes
Prevention: Identify and avoid triggers, regular sleep schedule, stress management, hydration

Symptoms: Wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness, coughing (especially at night)
Description: A condition in which airways narrow, swell, and may produce extra mucus, making breathing difficult.
Treatment: Inhaled corticosteroids, bronchodilators (albuterol), leukotriene modifiers, biologics
Cause: Airborne allergens, respiratory infections, exercise, cold air, air pollutants, smoke
Prevention: Avoid triggers, use air purifiers, take preventive medication, get flu vaccinations

Symptoms: Chest pain, cough with phlegm, fever, chills, shortness of breath, fatigue
Description: Infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
Treatment: Antibiotics (bacterial), antiviral medications, fever reducers, rest, fluids, hospitalization if severe
Prevention: Vaccination (pneumococcal, flu), good hygiene, not smoking, healthy immune system

Symptoms: High fever, severe headache, pain behind eyes, joint and muscle pain, rash, mild bleeding
Description: A mosquito-borne viral infection common in tropical regions, causing flu-like illness.
Treatment: Supportive care — rest, fluids, paracetamol for fever; no specific antiviral
Cause: Aedes aegypti mosquito bite carrying dengue virus (4 serotypes)
Prevention: Use mosquito repellent, eliminate standing water, wear protective clothing, use bed nets

Symptoms: Cough lasting 3+ weeks, coughing blood, chest pain, weight loss, night sweats, fever
Description: A serious infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, primarily affecting the lungs.
Cause: Mycobacterium tuberculosis spread through airborne droplets
Prevention: BCG vaccination, early diagnosis, proper ventilation, completing full antibiotic course

Symptoms: Fever, chills, headache, nausea, vomiting, muscle pain, fatigue, sweating
Description: A life-threatening disease caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted through infected mosquito bites.
Treatment: Antimalarial drugs: artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs), chloroquine where effective
Cause: Plasmodium parasites (falciparum, vivax, malariae, ovale) via Anopheles mosquito
Prevention: Insecticide-treated bed nets, antimalarial prophylaxis, indoor spraying, mosquito control

Symptoms: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, dizziness, cold hands and feet
Description: A condition with insufficient healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to tissues.
Treatment: Iron supplements, vitamin B12/folate supplements, treating underlying cause, blood transfusion in severe cases
Cause: Iron deficiency, vitamin B12/folate deficiency, chronic disease, blood loss, bone marrow issues
Prevention: Balanced diet rich in iron and vitamins, treat underlying conditions, regular checkups

Symptoms: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal cramps, fever, muscle aches
Description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, commonly called stomach flu.
Treatment: Oral rehydration therapy, rest, bland diet (BRAT), antiemetics, antibiotics for bacterial causes
Cause: Viruses (norovirus, rotavirus), bacteria (Salmonella, E. coli), parasites, contaminated food/water
Prevention: Hand hygiene, safe food handling, clean water, rotavirus vaccination for children

Symptoms: Fatigue, weight gain, cold sensitivity, constipation, dry skin, hair loss, depression
Description: Underactive thyroid — the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough thyroid hormone.
Treatment: Levothyroxine (synthetic T4 hormone), regular TSH monitoring
Cause: Hashimoto's thyroiditis, thyroid surgery, radiation therapy, iodine deficiency
Prevention: Adequate iodine intake, regular thyroid screening especially for women over 35

Symptoms: Fatigue, swelling in legs/ankles, decreased urination, nausea, shortness of breath, confusion
Description: Gradual loss of kidney function over time, leading to waste buildup in the body.
Treatment: Blood pressure control, diabetes management, low-protein diet, dialysis, kidney transplant
Cause: Diabetes, hypertension, glomerulonephritis, polycystic kidney disease
Prevention: Control blood sugar and blood pressure, healthy diet, avoid nephrotoxic medications

Symptoms: Persistent sadness, loss of interest, changes in appetite/sleep, fatigue, feelings of worthlessness
Description: A mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness and loss of interest, affecting daily activities.
Treatment: Antidepressants (SSRIs, SNRIs), psychotherapy (CBT), lifestyle changes, ECT for severe cases
Cause: Genetics, brain chemistry, trauma, chronic illness, stress, hormonal changes
Prevention: Regular exercise, social support, stress management, early treatment, avoiding substance use

Symptoms: Fever, cough, shortness of breath, fatigue, loss of taste/smell, body aches, sore throat
Description: Infectious disease caused by SARS-CoV-2, ranging from mild illness to severe respiratory disease.
Treatment: Supportive care, antivirals (Paxlovid, remdesivir for severe cases), oxygen therapy, ventilation
Cause: SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus spread via respiratory droplets and aerosols
Prevention: Vaccination, masking, hand hygiene, ventilation, social distancing, testing

Symptoms: Joint pain, stiffness, swelling, reduced range of motion, grating sensation
Description: Degenerative joint disease causing cartilage breakdown, most commonly in knees, hips, hands, and spine.
Treatment: Pain relievers (NSAIDs), physical therapy, weight management, corticosteroid injections, joint replacement
Cause: Aging, joint injury, obesity, genetics, repetitive stress on joints
Prevention: Maintain healthy weight, regular low-impact exercise, protect joints from injury
